sourceMovie.title: Crocodile Dundee
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Crocodile Dundee for the same reason it broke through in 1986: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Peter Faiman keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Rundown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Crocodile Dundee clicked for you, The Rundown (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Crocodile Dundee?
faq.answer: Try The Rundown, Rat Race, The Lobster—each shares DNA with Crocodile Dundee while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Crocodile Dundee?
faq.answer: Crocodile Dundee (1986) is best described as Adventure, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Crocodile Dundee part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Crocodile Dundee Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
